facts.shortSummary: At the Leviathan Inn, Rayna tells the party that the ocean's song has returned and begs them to recover a trapped fragment of it from a hidden shrine near Hamri.
facts.longSummary: The session opens with a broad recap of Hamri's recent history before Wazir gathers Ryu, Trok, Nerissa, and Ander at the Leviathan Inn to hear out a storm-battered sailor named Rayna. She explains that she has heard the ocean speak in visions since childhood, that the songs fell silent years ago after a nightmare of tearing and loss, and that only recently they returned with a plea about something trapped under rock yet still resting on the waves. The voice showed her a shrine of Guzo the Mariner and begged for a fragment of song to be restored, so she has come to Hamri asking for help from people she has been told can be trusted.

location.context: common room gathering as Wazir hears out a newly arrived sailor
location.notes: The whole opening scene stays inside Wazir's inn while Rayna explains why she came to Hamri.

npcs.name: Rayna
npcs.notes: She asks the party to recover the lost fragment and return it to its home.

npcs.name: Caleb
npcs.notes: He raised Rayna on stories of Mawar and was uneasy when the ocean's voice first went silent.

npcs.name: Kaeso
npcs.context: Chardonian scholar recently staying in Hamri
npcs.notes: He appears in the session recap of Hamri's recent troubles and rumors from Chardon.

npcs.name: Guzo the Mariner
npcs.context: Mawarin saint seen in Rayna's visions
npcs.notes: Rayna's picture of the hidden place centers on a shrine to Guzo.

items.name: fragment of song
items.notes: Rayna describes a lost fragment of the ocean's voice trapped somewhere in the cliffs near Hamri.

organizations.name: Ocean Watchers
organizations.notes: Rayna grew up on stories about them and Hamri's strange relationship with the sea.

facts.shortSummary: The party questions Rayna, consults Yaz and Ovina, and learns that the vision likely points to a long-buried shrine of Guzo in the sea caves of Hamri's North Cliff.
facts.longSummary: Still at the Leviathan, the group presses Rayna for more detail and works from Trok's sketch of the place shown in her vision. Yaz recognizes the picture as matching an old shrine of Guzo the Mariner once built in a sea cave on the North Cliff, while Ovina, an old dwarf who has been in Hamri for more than a century, remembers the cave and confirms it collapsed long ago near the last tower in the line. With their clues aligned around a buried coastal shrine and a likely low-tide approach, the party settles on taking Ryu's boat out in the morning to search the site directly.

location.context: extended question-and-answer session with local experts summoned to the inn
location.notes: The inquiry stays in the tavern while the party assembles enough local memory to identify the North Cliff shrine.

npcs.name: Rayna
npcs.notes: She confirms the blue-lit cave shrine and the need to recover the fragment.

npcs.name: Yaz
npcs.context: elderly local source on Hamri's older shrines
npcs.notes: He identifies the vision as matching a Guzo shrine once built in a North Cliff sea cave.

npcs.name: Ovina
npcs.context: long-lived dwarf blacksmith with old harbor memory
npcs.notes: She remembers the cave, places it near the last tower, and says it collapsed long ago.

npcs.name: Guzo the Mariner
npcs.context: saint honored by the lost shrine
npcs.notes: Both the sketch and the local testimony point to a cave shrine dedicated to Guzo.

items.name: Trok's shrine sketch
items.notes: The party uses Trok's drawing from Rayna's description to question Yaz and Ovina.

items.name: fragment of song
items.notes: The group's entire search is framed around recovering the trapped fragment Rayna described.

facts.shortSummary: The next morning the party sails to the North Cliff, finds the old collapse, and confirms that a blasted opening now leads back into the buried shrine cave.
facts.longSummary: After sleeping on the plan, the group meets the next morning, takes breakfast, and boards Ryu's small boat with Rayna along for the ride. They follow the North Cliff shoreline to the place Ovina described, use scouting and shared checks to study the rockfall, and realize that the cave has recently been punched open again by some force far cleaner than a natural collapse. With Ryu linked through Turk's senses and Wazir's mechanical fish scouting alongside, they confirm there is still a navigable way into the old shrine beyond the rubble.

location.to: North Cliff sea cave outside Hamri
location.context: morning boat trip and shoreline search for the collapsed Guzo shrine
location.notes: The beat ends once the party identifies the blasted passage back into the cave.

npcs.name: Rayna
npcs.context: sailor accompanying the search by boat
npcs.notes: She joins the ride out and helps confirm that the party has reached the place from her vision.

facts.shortSummary: Swimming through the breach, the party finds the blue-lit beach and ruined outer temple of Guzo's shrine, spots idle water creatures, and advances toward the building.
facts.longSummary: The party dives through the newly blasted underwater opening and surfaces inside a hidden sea cave whose interior matches Rayna's vision: a warm blue glow, a strip of sandy beach, and a white shrine building on the shore. They quickly realize the collapse was not the only recent disturbance, because the breach and the shattered doorway deeper inside both look as if magic blasted straight through stone and timber. A cluster of watery, frog-like shrine creatures loiters around the beach and temple, and after sizing up the scene the party presses inward instead of retreating for a longer plan.

location.primary: buried sea cave shrine of Guzo (North Cliff)
location.context: sandy inner beach and ruined outer temple beyond the blasted underwater passage
location.notes: This is the first direct entry into the hidden shrine cave Rayna saw in her vision.

items.name: shattered reinforced door
items.notes: Broken chunks of the inner doorway show that something recently blasted deeper into the shrine.

facts.shortSummary: Inside the shrine, the party uncovers books and offerings, finds the ancestor prayer that opens a hidden stone passage, and reveals a trapped water elemental clutching the crystal fragment.
facts.longSummary: With the outer approach secured for the moment, the party searches the main temple and the side chapel in earnest. They study the statue of Guzo, the glowing altar, scattered offerings, and a damaged stone chest that still preserves a prayer book, a saintly story collection, and a ledger of prayers left at the shrine. The Mawarin inscription about kneeling and praying Guzo's ancestor prayer leads Nerissa to the right text, and when she sings it the hidden slab door vanishes to expose a deeper chamber. Inside waits a large water elemental figure pinned by the shrine's blue light and holding the crystal fragment the party came to recover.

location.context: main sanctuary, side chapel, and newly revealed hidden chamber
location.notes: The beat tracks the party from temple investigation to the revelation of the trapped inner-chamber intruder.

npcs.name: Guzo the Mariner
npcs.context: saint honored by the shrine's altar, prayer, and inscriptions
npcs.notes: The hidden passage opens only through Guzo's ancestor prayer.

npcs.name: Gazankoa
npcs.context: power invoked by the trapped elemental's minion
npcs.notes: The elemental's servant blurts out that the crystal is meant for Gazankoa.

items.name: prayer book
items.notes: A preserved book in the chapel chest contains Guzo's ancestor prayer.

items.name: saints' story book
items.notes: An illuminated collection of local saint stories survives in the same stone chest.

items.name: prayer ledger
items.notes: A damaged guestbook preserves prayers and hopes once left at the shrine.

items.name: song fragment crystal
items.notes: The hidden chamber contains the crystal fragment the ocean wants restored.

facts.shortSummary: The trapped elemental offers to spare the party if they free it and let it take the crystal to Gazankoa, but the blue light's binding effect and the creature's threats convince the group to fight instead.
facts.longSummary: Before steel is drawn, the party talks with the elemental in the hidden chamber and learns how badly things have gone inside the shrine. The creature explains that it forced its way in, seized the crystal fragment, and triggered the blue light bound to the altar, which now prevents its teleportation and keeps it trapped despite the opened passage. It offers to return to the plane of water and even let the party present the crystal to Gazankoa, but the more it talks the clearer it becomes that it will simply keep hunting the shard and anyone carrying it. By the end of the exchange, the party settles on bottling the creature up inside the shrine and killing it rather than trusting any bargain.

location.context: threshold between the glowing altar room and the elemental's prison chamber
location.notes: The whole beat is a tense negotiation at the doorway before initiative begins.

npcs.name: Gazankoa
npcs.context: the power the elemental claims to serve
npcs.notes: The elemental frames the crystal as something that should be brought to Gazankoa.

items.name: song fragment crystal
items.notes: The crystal remains in the elemental's grasp during the negotiation and drives the whole argument.

facts.shortSummary: The elemental opens the fight with a brutal jet of water, hurling the party back, dropping Geo, and turning the doorway ambush into a hard scramble for control.
facts.longSummary: When the parley breaks down, initiative starts and the trapped elemental wastes no time, lashing the shrine interior with a massive jet of frigid water. The opening blast batters Trok, Ryu, Ander, Geo, and the nearby shrine creatures, immediately showing how dangerous the foe is even while confined by the blue light. Ryu recovers enough to answer with thrown trident strikes and Hunter's Mark, Ander fights his way back into the doorway, and the party's neat plan to contain the creature turns into a messy opening brawl in the temple.

location.context: altar room and doorway to the hidden chamber during the opening combat exchange
location.notes: The fight begins with the party clustered near the threshold and stairs while the elemental attacks from deeper inside.

mainEnemies.name: trapped water elemental
mainEnemies.notes: The crystal-holding intruder trapped by the shrine's blue light opens the fight with overwhelming force.

mainEnemies.name: shrine water creatures
mainEnemies.notes: The lesser watery creatures around the shrine support the elemental during the first rounds.

combat.notes: The opening rounds are defined by the elemental's water jet, the party's disrupted formation, and the sudden realization that the fight will not be quick.

facts.shortSummary: The shrine's blue light begins to work against the elemental, and Nerissa's magic drives it back while the lesser creatures crowd the stairs and start grappling the front line.
facts.longSummary: As the melee settles into its middle phase, the same blue glow binding the elemental starts actively helping the party. Nerissa's dissonant whispers bite especially hard under the altar's influence and force the elemental backward into the chamber, where opportunity attacks from Trok and Wazir leave it staggering and badly wounded. The lesser shrine creatures rush the stairs and doorway, hardening their bodies and then grappling Ryu and Ander with watery scimitars, but the party keeps pressure on the main foe until it is below a quarter of its strength and ready to change tactics.

location.context: altar platform, doorway, and stair choke point as the party drives the elemental back and the minions press forward
location.notes: The blue-lit sanctuary itself becomes part of the party's advantage during this phase.

mainEnemies.name: trapped water elemental
mainEnemies.notes: The main foe is bloodied, then pushed below a quarter of its strength, while visibly hindered by the shrine's blue light.

mainEnemies.name: shrine water creatures
mainEnemies.notes: The lesser creatures surge forward, harden defensively, and begin grappling Ryu and Ander at the stairs.

combat.notes: The middle turns when the shrine's magic and Nerissa's spell combine to drive the elemental back, even as its minions pin down the front line.

facts.shortSummary: The wounded elemental turns to mist, slips through the wall, punishes fleeing minions, and is finally destroyed before the party cuts down the last shrine creatures.
facts.longSummary: The final phase begins when the badly wounded elemental abandons a straight fight and turns into watery mist to move away from the party. Ryu and Ander kill grappling minions while Wazir's shatter and homunculus attacks help thin the field, and several lesser creatures start fleeing for the water. The boss reappears outside the chamber, lashes out at Ander and Trok, and even kills one of its own fleeing servants for cowardice, but Trok redirects one of its whip strikes and Ryu marks it before a trident blow makes the elemental collapse into a puddle. In the next few minutes, the party kills the last two creatures before they can swim off and threaten Rayna.

location.primary: Guzo shrine inner temple and sea cave beach
location.context: altar room, wall-side escape route, stairs, and beach as the fight spills toward the water
location.notes: This beat follows the boss's attempted mist-form escape through the wall and the final cleanup of fleeing creatures.

mainEnemies.name: trapped water elemental
mainEnemies.notes: The fleeing elemental is destroyed after turning to mist, reappearing outside the chamber, and trying to rally or punish its servants.

mainEnemies.name: remaining shrine water creatures
mainEnemies.notes: The last lesser creatures grapple, flee, and are eventually killed before they can swim off after Rayna.

combat.notes: The combat ends once Ryu's marked trident strike collapses the elemental and the party finishes the final two creatures.

facts.shortSummary: After the fight, Nerissa hears the recovered crystal's grateful song, the party returns it to Rayna, and Rayna accepts the task of carrying it to Omni.
facts.longSummary: With combat over, the party gathers around the crystal and Nerissa hears a faint melody of peaceful sleep and quiet gratitude. A quick search confirms that the shrine is mostly ruined and flooded, with little left beyond the offerings Trok already looted, so the group leaves the cave and swims back to Rayna. When they hand her the fragment, everyone shares a vision of an undersea home being rebuilt among kelp and fishfolk; Rayna recognizes the destination and says the crystal can guide her. The party returns to the Leviathan Inn that afternoon, helps Rayna settle in for one more night, and learns she means to sail across the Mawar Ocean toward Apporia and the merfolk city of Omni in the morning.

location.context: post-combat shrine search, crystal handoff at Rayna's boat, and return to town
location.notes: The beat begins after the last creatures are dead and ends with Rayna settled at the Leviathan before sailing for Omni.

npcs.name: Rayna
npcs.context: sailor entrusted with the recovered crystal
npcs.notes: She accepts the fragment, recognizes the vision of its destination, and plans to carry it toward Omni.

npcs.name: Guzo the Mariner
npcs.context: saint honored by the ruined shrine offerings
npcs.notes: The party confirms there is little left in the shrine beyond offerings once left for Guzo.

npcs.name: Gazankoa
npcs.context: larger conflict named in the closing epilogue
npcs.notes: The DM frames Hamri's restored normalcy as still disturbed by the long-running conflict involving Gazankoa, the plane of water, and the sentient ocean.

items.name: song fragment crystal
items.notes: The recovered crystal sings gratitude, shows the party a vision of its undersea home, and guides Rayna toward Omni.

items.name: Guzo shrine offerings
items.notes: The party notes that Trok already looted the old offerings left in the ruined shrine.
